title: harness capability manifest document
description: Canonical form of a harness capability manifest: the capability set one harness node provides to a CodegenBundle, under a versioned ABI. tools/codegen_bundle.py holds the manifests as tool-side data (HARNESS_CAPABILITY_MANIFESTS), renders them in this document form (harness_capability_manifest_document), and validates them against this shape (harness_capability_manifest_violations); the harness controlled_spec.md is NOT edited to carry them, because that would recertify a certified artifact for no change in generated behavior. When the harness spec is next re-specified on content grounds (Z6, docs/design/zero_base_architecture.md), the manifest moves into a spec-side capabilities section of exactly this shape. Negotiation matches an exact name@version token: version ordering never implies compatibility, and an undeclared harness provides nothing (fail-closed).

harness_capability_abi_version.description: The ABI generation this manifest document is written against. Pinned, not a floor: this contract is generation HARNESS_CAPABILITY_ABI_VERSION = 1, and a later-generation manifest is rejected rather than read under generation-1 semantics (forward compatibility is never assumed — the same rule the exact name@version token match applies to a single capability).

manifests.description: One entry per harness node_key. A harness_capability_abi_version-compatible consumer treats an absent node_key as providing nothing.

node_key.description: The harness node this manifest describes. Only an infrastructure node provides capabilities.

provides.description: The capability tokens this harness provides, duplicate-free. sync_single_case@1 is defined as exactly the canonical interface block of harness_fortran_cpu@0.3.0 section 5.1 (13 operations, 5 published types, dp = real64, case_id_len = 64); tools/runner_renderer.py:assert_harness_pin remains its mechanical enforcer.

capability_token.description: <capability_name>@<abi_version>. The name must be a member of the closed vocabulary below; the version is an exact integer, never a range.

x-forbidden-examples.note: a capability name outside the closed vocabulary
a version range; only an exact integer version is a token
a bare name with no ABI version
only an infrastructure node provides capabilities

x-forbidden-examples-note: This schema rejects on its own: a version range and a bare name (the token pattern), and a non-infrastructure node_key (the node_key pattern). A well-formed token whose NAME is outside x-capability-vocabulary (gpu_magic@1) matches the token pattern and is rejected by the canonical validator, tools/codegen_bundle.py:harness_capability_manifest_violations, which holds the vocabulary as a closed set.
